name: Brazilian Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: ½ cup Unsalted butter, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Use a tube pan made of one piece; it is essential because the batter is very thin and will flow through seams of a two-piece pan.
3. Generously butter the pan, then coat with flour. Tap out excess flour.
4. Measure all ingredients.
5. Melt the chocolate using 2 tablespoons hot coffee over low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
6. Cream the butter; gradually add the sugar and beat until light.
7. Add eggs one at a time, then stir in vanilla extract and the melted chocolate.
8. Sift the flour into two cups. To this, add baking soda and salt.
9. Gently stir the dry ingredients into the batter, alternating with coffee, ensuring everything is well incorporated.
10. Pour the batter into the prepared tube pan and bake for 50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Let rest 5 minutes before turning out onto a rack to cool completely.
12. Dust with powdered sugar. The cake can be served on its own or filled with chocolate icing.
